methods.description: This method describes the procedure for analyzing samples, likely containing fatty acids, using Gas Chromatography coupled to an Ion Trap Mass Spectrometer (GC-IT/MS). It details the specific instrument configuration, operational parameters for the GC and MS, temperature programming, data acquisition mode, and compound identification strategies based on retention data, mass spectra comparison with standards, and database searching, as well as retention index calculation and comparison.

steps.description: Samples were analyzed using a Varian CP-3800 gas chromatographer coupled to a Varian Saturn 4000 mass selective ion trap detector (Palo Alto, CA, USA) and a Saturn GC-MS workstation software version 6.8. A VF-5 ms (30 m × 0.25 mm × 0.25 µm) column (Varian) and a CombiPAL automatic auto sampler (Varian) were used.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure GC-IT/MS instrument hardware and software setup.
inputs: Varian Saturn 4000 mass selective ion trap detector
VF-5 ms (30 m × 0.25 mm × 0.25 µm) column

steps.description: The injector port was heated to 250 °C. Injections were performed in split mode, with a ratio of 1/40. The injection volume was 2 µL.
steps.searchable_summary: Set GC injector parameters including temperature, mode, ratio, and volume.

steps.description: The carrier gas was helium C-60 (Gasin, Leça da Palmeira, Portugal), at a constant flow of 1 mL/min.
steps.searchable_summary: Set GC carrier gas parameters including type and flow rate.

steps.description: The ion trap detector was set as follows: transfer line, manifold and trap temperatures were 280, 50 and 180 °C, respectively. The emission current was 50 µA and the electron multiplier was set in relative mode to auto tune procedure. The maximum ionization time was 25.000 µs, with an ionization storage level of 35 m/z.
steps.searchable_summary: Set MS ion trap detector parameters including temperatures, emission current, multiplier, and ionization settings.

steps.description: The mass ranged from 50 to 600 m/z, with a scan rate of 6 scan/s. The analysis was performed in Full Scan mode.
steps.searchable_summary: Set MS scan parameters including mass range, scan rate, and scan mode.

steps.description: The oven temperature was set at 100 °C for 1 min, then increasing 20 °C/min to 250 °C and held for 2 min, 10 °C/min to 300 °C and held for 10 min.
inputs: Temperature program settings (initial temp/hold, ramps, final temp/hold)

steps.description: The injection volume was 2 µL and the analysis was performed in Full Scan mode.
steps.searchable_summary: Inject sample into the GC system.

steps.description: All mass spectra were acquired in electron impact (EI) mode. Ionization was maintained off during the first 4 min to avoid solvent overloading.
steps.searchable_summary: Run GC-IT/MS analysis with specified parameters, acquire mass spectra in EI mode, and apply ionization delay.

steps.description: Compounds were identified by comparison of retention times and MS fragmentation pattern with those of standards analyzed under the same conditions...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify compounds by comparing retention times and MS fragmentation patterns with standards.
inputs: MS fragmentation patterns of known standards
outputs: List of identified compounds based on standard comparison

steps.description: ...and a mass spectra database search was performed using the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) MS 05 spectral database.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform mass spectra database search for compound identification.
outputs: List of potential compound identifications with match scores from database search

steps.description: In addition, the retention index (RI) was experimentally calculated... For the RI determination, an n-alkanes series (C8-C40) was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Experimentally calculate retention indices using n-alkane standards.
inputs: Raw GC data for sample and n-alkane series (C8-C40)
Retention times of compounds and n-alkanes

steps.description: ...and the values were compared with those reported in the literature for GC columns with 5%-phenyl-95%-dimethylpolysiloxane [33,34].
steps.searchable_summary: Compare experimental retention indices with literature values for validation.
inputs: Literature retention index values for 5%-phenyl-95%-dimethylpolysiloxane columns
outputs: Validation status of compound identifications based on RI comparison
